name: Susiwati Ta
position: Product Manager with Payments and Ecommerce experience
about: With experience in product management and engineering, I am driven by delivering customer value and seeing my products making a positive impact in my user's lives. Right now, I am creating new payment experiences at PayPay, the largest mobile payment app in Japan. Working for the Japan market has been an interesting experience in many ways, and I particularly enjoy the special ownership mentality that the Japanese users has towards the services they use. Many of my projects are still work in progress, and I will update when they're out of the woods. Previously, I was a product manager at Shopee, the largest e-commerce platform in Southeast Asia and Taiwan. At Shopee, I own two products under the supply chain team: finance and inventory. I led the 0 to 1 product design, scoping, and phasing of a new system to calculate the fees payable by sellers to Shopee for their usage of Shopee’s services. I am also comfortable with handling complex requirements, having multiple stakeholder teams from 8 different business markets. I value-add by designing products that elegantly serve business requirements with a scalable technical framework. I am eager to explore new opportunities in the fast-growing and dynamic e-commerce industry. I am also eligible for the H-1B1 visa for the US and the EU blue card for Germany, which offer me flexibility and mobility to work in different markets and cultures. I am looking for a product management role that aligns with my mission of creating value for customers and businesses through innovative and scalable products.

experience.title: Product Manager (Supply Chain)
experience.description:
Shopee is the largest e-commerce platform in Southeast Asia and Taiwan, with USD18.2 billion in gross merchandise value in 2021. I owned 2 products (Finance and Inventory) under the Supply Chain team. I built strong working relationships with multiple stakeholder teams from 8 business markets (SEA, TW, Brazil), and my developers in Shenzhen.


Product 1: Finance, specifically the calculation of shipping fees and fulfillment fees 		

◆      Led the 0 to 1 product design, scoping and phasing of a new system to calculate the fees payable by sellers to Shopee for their usage of Shopee’s services. Achieved 100% visibility of all fees in major markets, with 80+% coverage in smaller markets, which will be done in phase 2.

◆      Drove the UAT, live testing, and launch of the new finance system with 99.5+% accuracy. Managed project timeline closely with cross-regional stakeholders (engineering, operations, finance) to achieve target accuracy by launch date.


Product 2: Inventory Stock Control in Shopee Fulfillment Network				

◆      Created new functionality for the inventory control system, to support evolving business models like multi-warehouses fulfillment, cross docking and drop shipping.

◆      Led the design of user-facing inventory visibility tools to completely eliminate manual communications of stock counts. 

◆      Scoped, proposed and led the development of inventory management features to guide replenishment decisions.

projects.title: Air Transportation Visualization
projects.description: This project aims to provide a holistic view of the volume of air traffic around various airports in the world. By utilizing data visualization tool such as Cesium.js, we aim to provide an easy and visual way to indicate traffic density as well as provide insights to potential aviation routes/plans.

projects.title: Hotel Demand Forecast
projects.description: Hotels fall under two categories, gazetted and non-gazetted hotels. Currently, STB only has a forecast model for visitors staying in gazetted hotels. Our aim is to find the international and domestic visitor occupancy of both gazetted and non-gazetted hotels to forecast ‘overall room nights occupied’. The first step in our methodology was to consolidate the data provided by STB using SQLite. Then, we were able to determine the additional data that we had to source out for our forecast models which we acquired by web scraping Airbnb data using python. Following that, we developed a prediction model to predict the ratio of domestic to international visitors in non-gazetted hotels. After collating STB’s data and the data that we sourced, we built our individual forecast models on R and implemented this code into our final interactive JavaScript visualisations. By doing so, we can provide STB with a detailed breakdown of overall hotel room demand into the resulting four categories. This will result in detailed and accurate forecasting; which can be used to make precise and effective business decisions.

projects.title: Visual Design of the Comprehensive Intelligent Large screen
projects.description: In this project, our group developed a large-screen visualisation for BoB under the mentorship of CADCG. The visualisation, slated for use in Bank of Beijing’s Nanjing branch, displays the volumes of data collected by the bank in an intuitive manner to enable the discovery of insights from an otherwise impenetrable screen of data. This large-screen visualisation in the monitoring center of their Nanjing branch is a foray into the relatively new field of big data analytics and will be used by bank leaders and business analysts to observe trends in bank and user data.

recommendations: Marie Therese Siew “Aditya worked with me on a research project on human in the loop learning from May-Nov 2024. He did an impressive job, which lead to a research publication! Firstly, he showed a lot of initiative in searching for, picking out, and going through relevant academic papers useful for our project. Secondly, he was very quick in understanding difficult concepts and implementing machine learning algorithms via code. Thirdly, he was creative and exploratory, in being able to draw links across different concepts and scenarios, and in ideating and proposing many potential algorithmic solutions for the different scenarios that we were modelling. The result is that, our research paper (where Aditya is the first author) was accepted at the AI4WCN workshop, held at the AAAI 2025 conference! I believe that with his initiative, his strong exploratory skills, and his ability to produce high quality work, Aditya will add value to whichever organization he joins. I highly recommend him for any role!”

projects.title: Product Design Studio - EV interior
projects.description: In partnership with the EV team at the Singapore University of Technology and Design (SUTD), I had the privilege of collaborating on the development of a functional and visually appealing interior for an electric vehicle (EV). Our joint efforts resulted in the design and implementation of a user-friendly steering wheel, dashboard, and back screens, enhancing both functionality and overall driving experience. It was an enriching experience working with the talented team at SUTD, and I am proud of the outcome we achieved together.

projects.title: AI Applications in Design - Finding design

projects.title: agentic product researcher
projects.description: This is an AI-powered product research tool built for a school project, exploring the use of emerging technologies like Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and AI Agents.The core idea is simple:You input an image of a product, and the tool runs a pipeline that collects reviews and feedback for that product and its competitors. The collected data is then processed into meaningful, actionable insights.Agents are employed to validate and extract relevant information from official product manuals, which are embedded into a vector database. These manuals, combined with user feedback, are used in a RAG system to generate targeted design improvement suggestions — for example, specific changes like "Increase maximum table height from 70 cm to 76 cm."The code was designed to execute end-to-end from app.py. While it currently functions as a proof-of-concept prototype, some bugs remain, and full flawless execution may require additional patches.

projects.title: Final Year Capstone Project: QueueCraft - Digital Twin Framework Development for Home Team Operations with Home Team Science and Technology Agency (HTX)
projects.description: • Organized a kick-off meeting with our client, HTX, to collect their objectives on building a digital twin which simulates physical queuing processes in the immigration hall virtually for better monitoring and optimization.• In Computer Vision part contributed Person Re-Identification Method using Omni-Scale Network model through Python to recognize the same person across different cameras which facilitates queuing pattern detection.• In Simulation part contributed Discrete-Event Simulation model through Python to simulate the live customer queuing and counter service processes and output live-updated queue length, number of served customers, etc. for monitoring.• Connected contributed Computer Vision and Simulation models with other teammates’ achievements to build the digital twin product, QueueCraft, which was later shown to HTX clients through presentation and poster showcase.

projects.title: Term Project: Advanced Topics in Stochastic Model - Use of Regenerative Simulation Method for Staffing Call Centers
projects.description: • Determined the regeneration epochs to conduct the regeneration cycles' simulation of the entire process of customers' arriving, call center agents' serving and customers' leaving in Python later.• Used Python programming language and a process-based simulation Python framework called SimPy to simulate the entire Call Center regeneration cycles' process.• Decided the expected minimum number of call center agents in steady-state and its confidence interval by meeting professor's constraints on probability to abandon customers and customer average wait time, so that the Call Center was staffed optimally and the project goal was met.(Since my project Python code and Report contain confidential information which may be used improperly by others, they will not be uploaded here and if you want to know more of my project details or want to see my project Python code or Report, please contact me.)

projects.title: Term Project: Simulation Modelling and Analysis​ Project - Restaurant Simulation
projects.description: • Used draw.io to plot the entire simulation flow in the restaurant including Pre-Ordering Phase, Kitchen Phase and Eating Phase for better visualization of the entire restaurant flow and later use in JaamSim software.• Simulated the whole event graph plotted in draw.io in a dynamic way and calculated the average queueing time for customers using JaamSim software for getting insights of how the customer queuing time could be improved.• Used JaamSim and Excel plot to find out that increasing customer interarrival time, decreasing cooking time, decreasing eating time and decreasing cashier service time could reduce the customer queuing time, which met our target of optimizing the restaurant simulation.

projects.title: Term Project: Educational Game for Optimal Stocking Plan - Supermarket Mania

publications.title: Accuracy-Risk Trade-Off Due to Social Learning in Crowd-Sourced Financial Predictions
publications.subtitle: Entropy
publications.description: A critical question relevant to the increasing importance of crowd-sourced-based finance is how to optimize collective information processing and decision-making. Here, we investigate an often under-studied aspect of the performance of online traders: beyond focusing on just accuracy, what gives rise to the trade-off between risk and accuracy at the collective level? Answers to this question will lead to designing and deploying more effective crowd-sourced financial platforms and to minimizing issues stemming from risk such as implied volatility. To investigate this trade-off, we conducted a large online Wisdom of the Crowd study where 2037 participants predicted the prices of real financial assets (S&P 500, WTI Oil and Gold prices). Using the data collected, we modeled the belief update process of participants using models inspired by Bayesian models of cognition. We show that subsets of predictions chosen based on their belief update strategies lie on a Pareto frontier between accuracy and risk, mediated by social learning. We also observe that social learning led to superior accuracy during one of our rounds that occurred during the high market uncertainty of the Brexit vote.

publications.title: Economic outcomes predicted by diversity in cities
publications.subtitle: EPJ Data Science
publications.description: Much recent work has illuminated the growth, innovation, and prosperity of entire cities, but there is relatively less evidence concerning the growth and prosperity of individual neighborhoods. In this paper we show that diversity of amenities within a city neighborhood, computed from openly available points of interest on digital maps, accurately predicts human mobility (“flows”) between city neighborhoods and that these flows accurately predict neighborhood economic productivity. Additionally, the diversity of consumption behaviour or the diversity of flows together with geographic centrality and population density accurately predicts neighborhood economic growth, even after controlling for standard factors such as population, etc. We develop our models using geo-located purchase data from Istanbul, and then validate the relationships using openly available data from Beijing and several U.S. cities. Our results suggest that the diversity of goods and services within a city neighborhood is the largest single factor driving both human mobility and economic growth.

projects.title: Predictive Analytics of Choice of Car Safety Feature

projects.title: Investigation of Similarity of Songs in Pop Culture via Network Analysis
projects.description: • Collaborated with industry partner Chordify, a music software company, to research on the degree of similarity among modern pop music from a purely analytical perspective.• Modelled 250 songs from Billboard Hot 100 as a directed network in Python, with nodes representing chords and edges representing chord progressions.• Investigated the level of similarity through the idea of musical chord progressions and utilized Degree, Eigenvector and Katz measures of centrality to prove that most pop music from the past 3 years followed an identical 4-chord progression.

projects.title: Stochastic Optimization of Sandwich Delivery Service
projects.description: •Established project scope for the optimization of a delivery system for Breadyard, a localsandwich café.• Modelled the inter-arrival times of orders to an exponential distribution through the use of Pearson’s chi-squared test andKolmogorov-Smirnov Test and modelled the delivery system as a M/G/1 queue system.• Programmed and executed Monte Carlo simulations in Python for alternative models of delivery system to determine theoptimal delivery policy in terms of cost and delivery time trade-offs.• Presented the project to the President of Singapore, Dr Tony Tan, during the President’s visit to the University in November2015.

projects.title: Investment Portfolio Simulator
projects.description: Our project consists of an interactive investment portfolio simulation tool designed to assist portfolio managers and wealth accumulation companies in crafting strategic investment strategies for their clients. The core of our project revolves around an interactive simulation game that emulates the dynamics of real-world investing over a 40-year span. This simulation is engineered to cater to different risk profiles, including risk-averse and high-risk takers, thereby allowing players to understand and craft tailored investment strategies that align with the investor's risk tolerance. It models an agent-based simulation approach, where stock returns are the agents, responding to various market events.We were motivated to pursue this project idea by taking up the ESD Course: 40.240 Investment Science. We wanted to apply the knowledge learnt in the Investment Science course to real world data and simulate the expected returns generated by risk-averse and high-risk portfolio allocation strategies. This project is also extremely useful for the wealth management industry as the simulation provides insightful results into the differences in returns generated upon employing different strategies of asset allocation.

projects.title: [60.003] Product Design Studio - Nika Atlas
projects.description: This Project was done as part of our Product Design Studio module at Singapore University of Design and Technology in partnership with Nika.Atlas is an innovative hybrid VTOL drone solution engineered to overcome real-world constraints in geospatial mapping missions. Designed for vertical takeoff and fixed-wing cruise, Atlas eliminates the need for traditional runway space while enhancing flight efficiency and data acquisition. With a focus on modularity, endurance, and adaptability, Atlas streamlines operations in constrained environments, empowering teams with a smarter, more flexible approach to aerial intelligence.Problem Statement: How might we increase flight duration, while ensuring ease of launching and landing as well as portability for efficient geospatial data collection?- VTOL drones are able to take off and land within constrained spaces, but have low flight endurance- Fixed-wing drones are able to sustain longer flights but require a runwayIn order to address these problems, we came up with Atlas as a solution.Project Done By:Aderic ChooAmit SankeIsaac OhJatlyson AngRittambhra Rani

projects.title: [03.007] Design Thinking and Innovation - EduCUBE
projects.description: This Project was done as part of our Design Thinking and Innovation module at Singapore University of Design and Technology.EduCUBE is a revolutionary tool designed to transform the classroom environment, enhancing student learning experiences and interactions. With a focus on optimizing student-student, student-speaker, and student-teaching assistant interactions, EduCUBE addresses common challenges students face in maintaining attention and engagement in class.Problem Statement: How Might We Enhance Student’s Learning Experience in Classroom?- Students are not able to keep up with/ understand the lesson materials.- Students may also be afraid to ask questions- Students are not being engaged enough by the professor.- Students are occupied with other things such as eating or doing other module homeworks.- Students cannot see what is presented on the board/screen at the front.In order to address these problem statements, we came up with Educube as a solution.Hardware Components:3x ESP32-S3,6x ILI9341 TFT 2.4 Inch Display,1x MPU6050 Gyroscope and AccelerometerLibraries Used:Bodmer's TFT eSPI - https://github.com/Bodmer/TFT_eSPIBodmer's TFT eWidgets - https://github.com/Bodmer/TFT_eWidgetAdafruit's MPU6050 - https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_MPU6050Project Done By:Angeline HalimJatlyson AngBrandon KimNicole NgTok Jing Huan

projects.title: [10.014] Computational Thinking for Design - Mini Arcade

The goal was to allow students to distress through a series of mini-games created using our newfound knowledge of Python to create a better world by design. We plan to have these games displayed in the area above the Indoor Sports Hall where the food vending machines are. Allowing students to relax when they crave some food after burning the midnight oil.This arcade machine would contain 4 different mini-games. Snake, Space Shooter, Lumberjack and Air Hockey. When the Python file is run, the user can choose one out of these 4 games to be played.Project Done By:Jatlyson AngBrandon KimZachary LeeTok Jing HuanAlson TayBrian Lim

projects.title: Heuristics and Systems Theory
projects.description: Project I: Applied Genetic Algorithms to optimize casino floor layouts, then simulated a mini-model to test and validate the solution’s performance.Project II: Used systems theory to analyze a blood glucose regulation model, examining equilibrium points and assessing system stability.

projects.title: Manufacturing and Service Operations Project
projects.description: Participated in a Lean LEGO production simulation comparing push (forecast-driven) and pull (demand-driven) systems to evaluate their impact on workflow efficiency, inventory levels, and costs. The project demonstrated that pull-based operations significantly reduce waste and improve responsiveness when supported by clear communication and coordinated team processes.

projects.title: Bayesian Regression & Predictive Modeling Project
projects.description: As part of SUTD's 40.017 Probability and Statistics module, our team applied Bayesian linear regression and bivariate normal distributions to analyze real-world datasets. We derived posterior and predictive distributions, performed prior sampling, and visualized uncertainty in predictions. This project deepened our understanding of probabilistic inference, with practical applications in decision-making under uncertainty.

projects.title: Optimization Project
projects.description: Formulated and solved a real-world decision problem using mixed-integer linear programming (MILP). Modeled constraints, defined objective functions, and validated solution correctness. Used JuMP in Julia to run simulations and interpret results for strategic optimization.

projects.title: Neighbourhood-Based Traditional Chinese Medicine for Healthy Aging

projects.title: Flood Adaptation Model

projects.title: Breakify
projects.description: As students experiencing similar pressures, we created Breakify, a one-stop platform designed to help students and working adults track their work and rest schedules. Breakify aims to alleviate pain from prolonged work sessions by reminding users to take breaks and optimizing their work-rest cycle based on past user data.Key Features:- Break-Work Timer: A minimalistic interface to help users manage their break and work sessions without distractions. Users can intuitively control and pause sessions if interrupted.- Social and Meetups: Users can add friends to go on breaks with and see which friends are also on breaks. Integration with Telegram allows for easy scheduling of meetups.- Activities: Utilizes the Google Maps API to generate walking routes to notable landmarks based on user-specified durations.

projects.title: ElderGPT
projects.description: While many elderly individuals own mobile phones, they often struggle to use various mobile applications due to their complexity. ElderGPT was created to address this issue by providing an elderly-friendly gateway to interact with multiple mobile applications.Overview:ElderGPT enhances usability for the elderly through the following core functionalities:- Text and Speech Interaction:Users can converse with the model using speech, supporting different languages via Streamlit.The model can read out its output using different agents with varying speaking rates, leveraging the ElevenLabs API.- Integration with Various Applications:The Langchain agent is equipped with custom tools for seamless integration with Google Calendar, Google Translate, Google Maps, News, Door Dash, and email.

projects.title: Erudite
projects.description: Have you ever faced existential crises and imposter syndrome from college workload? You're not alone! Keeping up with the pace expected by lecturers can be daunting. Erudite was created to help students learn better by providing structure and self-guided learning tools.Erudite enhances the learning experience through three main features:- Reducing Cognitive Leaps:Structures learning journeys with topical segregation and concept decomposition.Uses LLM capabilities to break down questions into digestible subsets, fostering critical thinking and problem-solving skills.- Topologically Sorting Topics:Generates dependencies between different course topics using LLM, allowing targeted revision.Helps users catch up on missed or lagging content by focusing on weak points.- Periodic Evaluation:Offers diagnostic evaluations to assess understanding and provide benchmarks.Guides users to further practices and resources based on their competency levels.

projects.title: greenflags
projects.description: When moving to the Bay Area, finding a place to live amidst San Francisco's high housing prices was a challenge. We've all heard the roommate horror stories, and the current solutions focus too much on the house itself rather than the people you live with. greenflags aims to solve this by focusing on personal compatibility and leveraging AI to ensure better roommate matches.greenflags is an AI-powered roommate matching service that enhances compatibility by understanding individual needs and preferences. Moving away from traditional form-based questionnaires, greenflags captures nuanced free-text responses to learn about users' personalities and desired roommate attributes.Key Features:- AI-Enhanced Matching: Uses AI to understand and match users based on detailed personality traits and preferences.- Compatibility Scoring: Provides compatibility scores, pentagon charts of overlapping traits, and AI representations of potential matches' responses.- User-Friendly Interface: A beautifully designed and accessible user interface that makes sharing personal stories easier and more comfortable.

projects.title: Jobpocalypse
projects.description: A retro 2D action shooter game where the player takes on the role of Timmy, a Computer Science graduate looking for a job. Timmy battles waves of HR recruiters with his resume, overcoming rejections to survive the tech winter. As he progresses, Timmy realizes the need to upgrade his skills beyond basic coursework to secure employment.Key Features:- Gameplay: Players control Timmy, fighting off HR recruiters in a survival scenario.- Skill Upgrades: Timmy must build industry-relevant skills to improve his chances of success.- Storyline: A narrative highlighting the importance of continuous learning and adaptation in the tech industry.

projects.title: LangUR
projects.description: Learning a language is challenging, and achieving fluency requires more than just proficiency. LangUR was developed to bridge the gap between basic language skills and true fluency. In a diverse business world, where hundreds of languages are used, fluency is crucial. Studies have shown that many language learners struggle to move beyond a beginner level, which motivated us to create a more effective learning solution.LangUR is a groundbreaking language learning platform that stands out through its efficiency, scalability, and practicality. By integrating language practice into the daily workflow, LangUR ensures consistent progress and fluency.Key Features:- Grounded Evaluation Metric: Uses the LIX scoring algorithm to measure performance.- Adaptive Learning: The application learns and adapts to individual learner's abilities.Seamless Integration: Incorporates language learning into the daily workflow, making it a natural part of the user's day.- Reinforcement: Reinforces learning through review quizzes and suggested articles based on past history.

projects.title: SingCast
projects.description: SingCast is a multi-functional web platform designed to provide accurate and easy-to-understand data visualizations. It addresses the needs of various Singtel stakeholders, including logistical support staff, ground staff, and campaign managers, by offering functionalities that aid in their daily operations. Key Features:- Dashboard: Visualizes store information with various filters for detailed insights.- Campaigns Page: Allows campaign managers to easily visualize and add campaigns.- Machine Learning Integration: Utilizes a trained Random Forest Regressor Model to forecast mobile phone demand based on various factors, helping logistical support staff plan ahead and maintain adequate supplies.

publications.title: Towards Understanding Emotions for Engaged Mental Health Conversations
publications.subtitle: DIS '24 Companion: Companion Publication of the 2024 ACM Designing Interactive Systems Conference
publications.description: Providing timely support and intervention is crucial in mental health settings. As the need to engage youth comfortable with texting increases, mental health providers are exploring and adopting text-based media such as chatbots, community-based forums, online therapies with licensed professionals, and helplines operated by trained responders. To support these text-based media for mental health–particularly for crisis care–we are developing a system to perform passive emotion-sensing using a combination of keystroke dynamics and sentiment analysis. Our early studies of this system posit that the analysis of short text messages and keyboard typing patterns can provide emotion information that may be used to support both clients and responders. We use our preliminary findings to discuss the way forward for applying AI to support mental health providers in providing better care.

publications.title: Designing AI-based Conversational Agent for Diabetes Care in a Multilingual Context
publications.subtitle: 25th Pacific Asia Conference on Information Systems: Information Systems (IS) for the Future (PACIS 2021)
publications.description: Conversational agents (CAs) represent an emerging research field in health information systems, where there are great potentials in empowering patients with timely information and natural language interfaces. Nevertheless, there have been limited attempts in establishing prescriptive knowledge on designing CAs in the healthcare domain in general, and diabetes care specifically. In this paper, we conducted a Design Science Research project and proposed three design principles for designing health-related CAs that embark on artificial intelligence (AI) to address the limitations of existing solutions. Further, we instantiated the proposed design and developed AMANDA - an AI-based multilingual CA in diabetes care with state-of-theart technologies for natural-sounding localised accent. We employed mean opinion scores and system usability scale to evaluate AMANDA’s speech quality and usability, respectively. This paper provides practitioners with a blueprint for designing CAs in diabetes care with concrete design guidelines that can be extended into other healthcare domains.

publications.title: Social Behaviour Understanding using Deep Neural Networks: Development of Social Intelligence Systems
publications.subtitle: Springer, Cham
publications.description: With the rapid development in artificial intelligence, social computing has evolved beyond social informatics toward the birth of social intelligence systems. This paper, therefore, takes initiatives to propose a social behaviour understanding framework with the use of deep neural networks for social and behavioural analysis. The integration of information fusion, person and object detection, social signal understanding, behaviour understanding, and context understanding plays a harmonious role to elicit social behaviours. Three systems, including depression detection, activity recognition and cognitive impairment screening, are developed to evidently demonstrate the importance of social intelligence. The study considerably contributes to the cumulative development of social computing and health informatics. It also provides a number of implications for academic bodies, healthcare practitioners, and developers of socially intelligent agents.

projects.title: Prioritisation of Echocardiography Requests
projects.description: This project was part of the 01.116 AI for Healthcare module in SUTD, and in collaboration with Sengkang General Hospital.The team developed machine learning models to classify and prioritise echocardiogram requests to optimise resource use and ensure timely care for critical patients, eventually achieving a maximum ROC AUC score of 0.672 with LightGBM, with key clinical features such as creatinine, troponin levels, and patient age identified as significant predictors. I worked on engineering 55 features from clinical data, including demographics, clinical history, and lab results; as well as machine learning models. SHAP and LIME were also used for global and local explainability of model predictions.

projects.title: Glowing Guide / Enhanced Patient Journey at Hospitals
projects.description: This project was part of the 01.400 Capstone 1 and 01.401 Capstone 2 modules at SUTD and was conducted in collaboration with Alexandra Hospital. The team focused on delivering personalised and optimised directional assistance within hospitals by scanning shoeprints and integrating projections with visual redesigns of signage and zones. My primary role in this project involved supporting the team's efforts in utilizing Amazon Web Services (AWS) for various components, including route optimisation, database setup and management, message and queue services, Internet-of-Things cloud service integration, and website hosting. I developed a website using React, Tailwind CSS, and AWS Amplify to simulate the proposed patient registration process. Additionally, I wrote AWS Lambda functions in Python and JavaScript to support various aspects of the project. My responsibilities also included maintaining the codebase and files for the entire project. To ensure the effectiveness of our design choices and improve system usability, the team conducted three user studies in the second term. Furthermore, in the first term, we conducted four user studies and interviews to understand the pain points experienced by patients, caregivers, and hospital staff. I also analysed results from our user studies in terms of qualitative analysis, quantitative analysis, and sentiment analysis, to propose evidence-based suggestions to inform the development of our final prototype.Throughout the project, I also contributed to the authoring of various written deliverables, including the project report, the content of the poster, the filming of the project video, the creation of content for the virtual showcase website, and the recording of meeting minutes.

projects.title: Glucose Conquest
projects.description: This project was done as part of the 01.114 Instructional Design of Serious Games for Healthcare module in SUTD, and in collaboration with KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital.The team developed a mobile-based virtual pet serious game designed to empower children dealing with Type 1 diabetes, and conceptualised game mechanics to teach young children about managing their health, learning vital skills such as taking glucometer readings and executing insulin injections. I was the main developer for this project, and worked on developing and coding the game using Unity and frontend game design screens developed on Figma.

projects.title: Guided by Love: A Caregiver’s Path
projects.description: This project was done as part of the 02.230TS Health Communication and Behaviour Change module in SUTD.The team conceptualised an interactive video designed to enhance understanding and empathy towards the experiences of caregivers, with the ultimate goal of promoting more comprehensive support systems. We eventually filmed a short trailer for this video. I helped to conceptualise the video direction and theories shown in the video, as well as written deliverables such as the report.

projects.title: PD-opoly
projects.description: This project was done as part of the 01.101 Technologies for Sustainable Global Health module in SUTD, in collaboration with the National Kidney Foundation (NKF).The team devised a way to reduce the knowledge gap of peritoneal dialysis (PD) among tertiary students in Singapore among and highlight it as an alternative treatment for kidney failure with the goal of fostering wider acceptance of PD in the future. We also conceptualised a new flow for NKF’s outreach sessions. I aided in the development of an interactive Figma prototype for a microsite that provided an enjoyable and immersive learning experience that could be done before, during, and after the session; as well as the design of a board game adapted from Monopoly to make the intricacies of PD accessible.

projects.title: Introduction To Neural Prostheses/Neural Assistive Devices
projects.description: This project was done under SUTD's Undergraduate Research Opportunities Programme. The aim was to build a research database that would enable the development of a prototype neural prosthesis that can successfully be integrated with the human nervous system through computer and brain interfacing.I conducted a comprehensive literature review on existing neural prostheses, developments in cognitive neuroscience, and cognitive science, identified the limitations or fallacies in existing research, and discussed and reviewed research articles related to the field of study with fellow team members.

projects.title: MindBridge
projects.description: This project was part of the CS374 Introduction to Human-Computer Interaction module in KAIST.In this project, the team proposed a Figma prototype aiming to redesign how working adults manage their mental health and wellbeing in their day-to-day lives. I designed user interfaces for the functional and interactive prototype using Figma to allow users to tap on existing social connections to encourage self-care and care for themselves like they would a virtual pet.

projects.title: Privacy Preservation Project
projects.description: This project was done as part of research in NCLab, a ubiquitous computing research lab under the supervision of Professor Junehwa Song in KAIST.In this project, I developed an Android application that aims to preserve the privacy of visually impaired people while others assist them in tasks. This application also utilised GPT-3.5 APIs to aid the censoring of sensitive information.

projects.title: Sterile Instrument Transporter
projects.description: This project was part of the CS470 Introduction to Artificial Intelligence module in KAIST.In this project, we developed a proof-of-concept robot to fully automate the distribution of sterilised medical instruments and the sterilisation visual checks in medical facilities. We utilised SLAM for mapping of hospital blueprint, navigation algorithms for transportation of sterile instruments from central sterile services departments to clinics, and anomaly and object detection to scan peel pouches to automate checking of received inventories.

projects.title: A Study on the Effects of Bold and Italics on the Memory of University Students
projects.description: This project was part of the 02.145TS Surveys and Experiments in the Social Sciences module in SUTD.As part of this project, I conducted literature review to understand relevant related theories, and conceptualised and prepared materials for the experiment. The team conducted an experimental study with 25 participants to determine if participants’ comprehension accuracy would be affected by bolding and italics of English passages.

projects.title: Online Hate Speech Detection
projects.description: Online Hate Speech DetectionThis project was part of the 50.007 Machine Learning module. In this project, we were required to train a variety of machine learning models for online hate speech classification. A competition was held on Kaggle amongst all students taking the course, and our group emerged top of the private leaderboard (80% of test data). Various machine learning methods and other techniques to improve model performance, especially those that were not taught in the course, were attempted. We also conducted extensive hyperparameter tuning to improve model performance.Our best performing model was the CatBoost Classifier, with an accuracy of 72.399%. Other models tried were: Random Forest Classifier with PCA, Grid Search and Adaboost, Bernoulli Naive Bayes, Support Vector Machine, Naive Bayes Support Vector Machine, Logistic Regression, LightGBM and XGBoost.

projects.title: TransferConnect
projects.description: This project was part of the 50.003 Elements of Software Construction module. TransferConnect acts as the middleman to facilitate the transfer and conversion of loyalty points from bank transactions, between participating loyalty programs and banks. For this project, we worked with Ascenda Loyalty.This project focuses on the development of a back-end API server and database for TransferConnect, as well as a front-end bank app to demonstrate the end-to-end workflow.Extensive testing for both the front-end and back-end and fuzzing were also implemented to ensure the proper functioning of the entire system. The system was implemented with the MERN stack.I developed the front-end bank app using React and Ant Design Pro, and wrote some simple front-end tests in Selenium. Express.js was also used to allow the front-end bank app to receive webhook notifications for transaction status updates. Express.js and Node.js were used in the development of the back-end API server. MongoDB was used in the development of the database. Jest was used for unit testing of the back-end.

projects.title: A Study on the Effects of Lo-Fi Music on the Ability to Recall Information
projects.description: This project was part of the 02.218 Introduction to Psychology module in SUTD.As part of this project, I conducted literature review to understand relevant related theories, and conceptualised and prepared materials for the experiment. The team conducted an experimental study with 40 participants to determine if context specificity would affect participants’ ability to recall and the number of errors in memory reconstruction.

projects.title: Pick Before Go
projects.description: This project was part of the 50.001 Information Systems & Programming module.Tired of going to Pick & GO only to find out that the item you want to buy is out of stock? Want to stay updated on promotions of the most popular items? Introducing the Pick Before Go app, a revamped user experience where users can get the most out of their Pick & GO experience. Opening the app brings you to our home screen, with the classic QR code for entry and a promotion carousel to view the current promotions in store. To browse through the product catalogue on your way to Pick & GO, simply use the search bar in the app to search for any product and tap on it. Not only will you be able to see its availability, you will also get to see a short description about it! You can even favourite items for quick access.But Pick & GO wouldn’t survive without the operational staff restocking the items everyday. With special administrator privileges, staff can also use the app as an inventory tool for operational tasks such as updating the promotions and availability of items. They can also easily add a new product if needed!With Pick Before Go, shopping at Pick & GO has never been easier and fuss-free!GitHub Link: https://github.com/kelliesyhh/SUTD-CSD-Term4-50.001-1DProject-PickBeforeGo

projects.title: Triangular Peg Solitaire
projects.description: This project was part of the 50.002 Computation Structures module. Peg Solitaire is a single-player strategy puzzle game that challenges your spatial and logical reasoning skills. Despite its seemingly simple set-up and rules, the game has racked the brains and brought entertainment to many since the 17th century.We have implemented the 15-holes triangle configuration of the Peg Solitaire game. The objective of the game is to eliminate pegs until there is only one peg left on the board. To remove pegs, you can jump over an adjacent peg into an empty hole, removing the adjacent peg.With 6816 solutions to the game, our game breaks down the original Triangular Peg Solitaire game into 3 levels of increasing difficulty. While attempting level 1 (3 starting pegs) and level 2 (7 starting pegs), you can learn the rules of the game and come up with your own strategies. You will then progress to the full version of the game in level 3 (14 starting pegs).3 colours are used to provide instant visual feedback. A blue light indicates remaining pegs, a green light indicates the peg currently selected, and a red light indicates the valid holes you can jump into. Unlit LEDs simply indicate a hole.GitHub Link: https://github.com/kelliesyhh/50002-1D-ALU

projects.title: Expressive Avatars To Reduce Social Anxiety In Therapy
projects.description: This project was done under SUTD's Undergraduate Research Opportunities Programme. This project aimed to realise avatar interaction as a viable alternative medium to deliver psychotherapy intervention.I assisted in the setting up and participation of a research study involving two 30 minute interactions with a counsellor, one with an avatar and one without an avatar. I was able to put myself in the shoes of both the researcher and research subject through this process.As part of the research process, I learnt statistical analysis tools such as JMP for data analysis in 4 weeks. This was coupled with my research on the scientific and technological aspects of Heart Rate Variability (HRV) measurement softwares to understand the biological/physiological relations to HRV as well as the scientific concepts behind the various calculations.

projects.title: Computational Tool for Biomedical Data Analysis
projects.description: This project was done under SUTD's Undergraduate Research Opportunities Programme. The project aimed to utilise machine learning to predict cardiac health from input data such as patient demographics, metabolomics data and exercises.A comprehensive literature review on existing projects involving machine learning in cardiac health was done over a period of 3 months to understand the current market and existing solutions. Following that, extensive pre-processing of the given dataset of 90 data points was conducted over a 3 week period. I used the multi-layer perception neural network to predict patients’ cardiac health with their demographics and current health conditions as predictor variables. Approximately 75% accuracy was achieved.

projects.title: Patient Output Inspection with AI
projects.description: Took up a project under SUTD's Undergraduate Research Opportunities Programme (UROP) in May 2020 prior to my first term in SUTD. The main aim of the project was to design AI and machine learning and image processing algorithm to perform automatic patient output inspection. All in all, I learnt how to implement Transfer Learning models, explored different methods for texture classification and got to see firsthand how AI could be used in healthcare fields other than diagnostics.

projects.title: SUTD Term 1 Simulator
projects.description: This game was designed for the 1D project component of the 10.014 Computational Thinking for Design module.This game takes the user through a series of scenarios, simulating a Freshmore student’s life through Term 1. At the start, the user is randomly assigned a character. While all characters have 4 attributes (Health, Money, Social and Intelligence), the initial values of each of these attributes are dependent on the assigned character. Throughout the game, in each scenario, the user’s responses determine the final values of their attributes. Once the game is run, the name of the assigned character, attributes and their respective values are displayed on the top right corner of the screen.A scenario is marked as such when the user is prompted with a question on screen, and is required to input their choice through the pop-up input box. As mentioned above, the attribute values are updated based on the user’s choice. Some of the choices will result in the activation of mini-games, which serve as bonus opportunities to boost the user’s attribute values. If the values of any attributes fall below 0 points, a particular set of revival questions will be activated, allowing the user to continue with the game if they meet certain conditions (e.g. having a certain other attribute value to be more than half of the initial attribute value). Should the user fail these revival conditions, they will be considered dead and the game will be over. At the end of the game, the user’s attribute values will be displayed on the screen, acting as a gauge of the user’s decision making skills.The main objective of the game is to not die before the final question pops up. Ideally, the user should have a balanced set of attribute values, showing that he/she is an all-rounded individual that is healthy, financially stable, decently sociable, and equipped with the right skills (i.e. high intelligence).This game was inspired by games like Reigns and ​Episode​ / ​Choices​.

projects.title: An Intelligent App that Cares for Your Mental Wellbeing / SG@Love App
projects.description: For Lion's Lair 2020, my team was one of 3 finalists. We pitched a remote and mobile assessment of mental health conditions since the psychological effects of COVID-19 have been felt and seen both globally and within Singapore. We developed a healthcare delivery system (through a mobile application) which is accessible not just to those with mental health conditions, but also to those who have never seen a psychiatrist before. We aimed to develop the first level of support for those who have mental health conditions and ease the burden on the already strained healthcare infrastructure.Through using a chatbot module, we allow for a conversational agent to be readily available for consultation. Utilising the National Speech Corpus developed by IMDA would increase the accuracy of the chatbot for locally accented English, thereby improving user engagement.Through the use of emerging technology such as AI, multiple modes of assessment can be offered to users, (eg: speech detection through audio recordings, image recognition, text detection).The app can also incorporate collective intelligence, leveraging on the collaborative efforts of users by gathering anonymous data regarding their mental states. This data can act as an enabler for many analytical applications, and can be harnessed to produce a public map of affected people. Governmental and mental healthcare institutions, can then utilise the map to identify hotspots for intervention, and to understand the mental status of the general populace.https://chiinnovate2020.klobbi.com/lionslair

projects.title: Fast Prototyping
projects.description: During my internship at A*STAR's Institute for Infocomm Research, I developed a web application to enable evaluations of novel language technologies for improving workflow efficiencies in a hospital telehealth setting.The web application was built with React and Flask. React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces that is maintained by Facebook and a community of individual developers and companies. Flask is a micro web framework written in Python that does not require particular tools or libraries. The design of the web application was made possible with Ant Design, a React UI library that contains a set of high-quality components and demos for building rich, interactive user interfaces. The database used for this project was Google Firebase.The web application consisted of 2 components: speech data component and structured data component. For the speech data component, users can submit a transcription of audio files (of a nurse-patient dialogue) in text format to be processed using the LSTM model. The results of the model will be displayed in a table. Users can also play audio files from a dropdown list and search for a specific word and display the sentence in which the word appears. For the structured data component, users can either enter structured data into 2 dataframes or upload files from their local directory. This will be processed by a machine learning model, and the results will be displayed in a table highlighting the most important feature and a time-series line graph showing risk levels.

projects.title: Depression Detection using Speech Analysis (Mobile App)
projects.description: Developed an Android application and a web server using Python. Learnt more about mental health and explored how technological advancements could be used in healthcare settings. A deep learning model was used to predict the probability of a user being depressed, while the web server linked the model and application.

projects.title: Mockup of a Eco-Friendly House
projects.description: Interacted with other ASEAN youths while building STEM skills through the development of a mock-up of a house with Micro:bit, sensors, LEDs, cardboard to solve a problem modelled after the United Nations’ Sustainable Development Goals (SDG13). Aimed to solve Philippines’ urban slums problem and introducing renewable energy and recycling and reducing waste habits.

projects.title: JAVERS: Just A Very Expressive Robot
projects.description: Developed a high-fidelity and low-fidelity prototype of an expressive cleaning robot using Arduino, Raspberry Pi and other electronic components through SUTD's 3-week Compact 3.007 Programme. The robot was targeted at elderly living at home alone and included cognitive games such as tic-tac-toe to prevent the onset of dementia. The low-fidelity prototype design was submitted to A’ Design Award and scored 85/100 for presentation and 5/10 for design.

projects.title: Mockup Application for Lomprayah
projects.description: A mockup of a mobile application was created by 5 Asian youths for Lomprayah (a Thai speedboat company) to improve user experience.
